growth_analysis.details: Revenue data incomplete; Positive FCFF growth; ROIC 25.9% (> 10 %)

risk_analysis.details: Beta NA; D/E 0.1; Interest coverage NA

macro_context.market_sentiment: Bullish risk-on mood on May 1: S&P 500 +0.54% to fresh all-time intraday high above 7,200, driven by Apple earnings beat (+3%) and geopolitical relief (Iran peace talks). April was the best month for S&P 500 and Nasdaq since 2020.
macro_context.fed_outlook: Fed held rates at 3.5%–3.75% on April 29 in a contentious 8-4 vote (most dissents since Oct 1992). Chair Powell ends term May 15 but stays as governor; Warsh nomination advancing. No cuts signaled near-term; stagnant labor market and elevated inflation (energy-driven) weigh on easing prospects.
macro_context.geopolitical: Middle East tensions (Iran, Strait of Hormuz threat) drove energy sector +40% YTD and kept inflation elevated; Iran engaging peace mediators as of May 1 pushed WTI down 2%. US tariffs continue to weigh on global manufacturing; IMF projects 2026 global growth at 3.1%, inflation at 4.4%. Fed leadership transition and US midterm elections add policy uncertainty.
key_events: Fed Chair Powell's last day as Chair: May 15, 2026 — Kevin Warsh likely successor
Ongoing Iran peace talks — oil price volatility risk
Big-tech Q1 earnings season wrapping up — AI capex trajectory in focus
US tariff policy developments — ongoing risk to supply chains and inflation

ticker_news.headline: Microsoft Q3 FY2026 beats: EPS $4.27 vs $4.06 est, revenue $82.89B vs $81.44B est; Azure +40% YoY; AI annual run-rate hits $37B (+123% YoY)
MSFT shares fell up to 5% post-earnings as Q4 capex guidance of $40B+ and full-year 2026 capex of $190B (~+61% YoY) spooked investors on ROI timeline
Microsoft amends OpenAI partnership: ends revenue-share payments; OpenAI gains right to sell AI models on rival clouds including AWS — reducing MSFT exclusivity
Microsoft 365 Copilot surpasses 20 million paid commercial seats; Azure AI services now $37B annualized run rate
Analyst downgrade: MSFT stock trading down ~3.9% after analyst downgrade on April 30, with multiple price target cuts following high capex guidance

analyst_consensus.recent_changes: Broadly bullish but with scattered cuts post-capex guidance: BMO lowered PT to $505 (Outperform maintained); BNP Paribas cut PT from $659 to $556 (Outperform); Cantor Fitzgerald cut PT from $590 to $502 (Overweight); Stifel downgraded to Hold with PT $392 (Feb 2026); Weiss Ratings cut from Buy to Hold (Mar 2026). Oppenheimer reaffirmed Outperform Apr 28. Consensus remains ~60 Buy / 2 Hold / 0 Sell across major aggregators (Strong Buy). PT range: $392–$675; avg ~$571.

web_context.competitor_activity: Google Cloud Q1 2026 grew 63% to $20B quarterly revenue (backlog ~$462B), leading Azure's 40% and AWS's 28% — Google emerging as most convincing AI ROI story per investor reaction. AWS+Google landing OpenAI model distribution after MSFT's exclusivity ended. Combined big-tech 2026 capex projected at $600–$725B. The cloud/AI arms race is intensifying, with MSFT facing credibility headwinds on its $190B capex ROI while Google's capex narrative was better received.
web_context.earnings_info: Most recent: Q3 FY2026 reported April 29, 2026 — EPS $4.27 (beat $4.06 est), revenue $82.89B (beat $81.44B est). Q4 FY2026 guidance: revenue ~$87.9B, EPS ~$4.28. Next earnings date: ~July 29, 2026 (Q4 FY2026); analyst EPS consensus ~$4.22. Capex guidance for Q4: $40B+; full-year 2026 capex: $190B.
